verdict: A highly energetic chart with strong rhythmic sync that unfortunately suffers from awkward 16th-note transitions and a physical 3-panel hold conflict.

issues.problem: HOLD D is active until 113.923, but a JUMP LR occurs at 113.558. This creates an illegal 3-panel requirement (D, L, R) forcing a player to drop the hold or use hands on a standard pad.
JUMP DR followed immediately by a 16th-note tap on L (91ms gap) forces an extremely awkward, jarring shift for the left foot.
JUMP DR followed immediately by a 16th-note tap on L creates another uncomfortable double-step transition similar to the one at 85.032.
JUMP LR followed immediately by a 16th-note tap on U is highly uncomfortable to execute cleanly at this speed.
